devanagari: दन्तवक्रविदूरथवधः बलरामद्वारा सूतशिरश्छेदश्च । अथाष्टसप्ततितमोऽध्यायः । श्रीशुक उवाच । शिशुपालस्य शाल्वस्य पौण्ड्रकस्यापि दुर्मतिः । पर...
english_devnagari: dantavakravidūrathavadhaḥ balarāmadvārā sūtaśiraśchedaśca athāṣṭasaptatitamo śrīśuka uvāca śiśupālasya śālvasya pauṇḍrakasyāpi durmatiḥ paralokagatānāṃ ca kurvanpārokṣyasauhṛdam

translation:
King Parīkṣit said:
I pay my obeisance to the Soul of the universe who through dint of his deluding potency brought about the destruction of the eighteen akṣauhiṇīs of army (in the Mahābhārata war) in equal number of days.

devanagari: एकः पदातिः सङ्क्रुद्धो गदापाणिः प्रकम्पयन् । पद्भ्यामिमां महाराज महासत्त्वो व्यदृश्यत ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: ekaḥ padātiḥ saṅkruddho gadāpāṇiḥ prakampayan padbhyāmimāṃ mahārāja mahāsattvo vyadṛśyata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: By listening to his stories and deeds in aggregate, my bewilderment, wonder and fear go on increasing again and again.

devanagari: तं तथायान्तमालोक्य गदामादाय सत्वरः । अवप्लुत्य रथात्कृष्णः सिन्धुं वेलेव प्रत्यधात् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tathāyāntamālokya gadāmādāya satvaraḥ avaplutya rathātkṛṣṇaḥ sindhuṃ veleva pratyadhāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: I consider that most probably my sinfulness which is difficult to cross has ceased to be. My senses are wide awake and the mind is extremely delighted and serene.

devanagari: गदामुद्यम्य कारूषो मुकुन्दं प्राह दुर्मदः । दिष्ट्या दिष्ट्या भवानद्य मम दृष्टिपथं गतः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: gadāmudyamya kārūṣo mukundaṃ prāha durmadaḥ diṣṭyā diṣṭyā bhavānadya mama dṛṣṭipathaṃ gataḥ
translation: My hunger and thirst have subsided. My soul (mind) is filled with ecstatic delight. It is through your grace, O expert in religion (religious discourse) that I feel that I have accomplished all my objects in this world.

devanagari: त्वं मातुलेयो नः कृष्ण मित्रध्रुङ्मां जिघांससि । अतस्त्वां गदया मन्द हनिष्ये वज्रकल्पया ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ mātuleyo naḥ kṛṣṇa mitradhruṅmāṃ jighāṃsasi atastvāṃ gadayā manda haniṣye vajrakalpayā
translation: I. however, wish to hear from you something more. When Balarāma found that his friends (both Kauravas and Pāṇḍavas) were bent on fighting and that he could not prevent them from doing so, he went to perform ablutions in sacred places. O sage rich in penance! Narrate to me what Balarāma did.

devanagari: तर्ह्यानृण्यमुपैम्यज्ञ मित्राणां मित्रवत्सलः । बन्धुरूपमरिं हत्वा व्याधिं देहचरं यथा ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tarhyānṛṇyamupaimyajña mitrāṇāṃ mitravatsalaḥ bandhurūpamariṃ hatvā vyādhiṃ dehacaraṃ yathā
translation: Being affectionate and loyal to my friends I shall pay off my debt (of friendship) to them by killing you, an enemy in the form of a cousin, just like a disease in one’s body, O ignorant fool.”[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā’s second interpretation of some adjectives in this verse:
ajña-(na vidyate jñaḥ yasmāt saḥ) Omniscient, Vyādhi—One who abides in the body as Inner Controller.
hatvā—Having arrived at the realization of your essential nature (han to go) ānṛṇyam upaimi: Pay off the debt of my ancestors (by realization of the Brahman).
“O Omniscient Lord! Due to Sanaka’s curse, I have become your cousin and enemy. But having realized your essential nature as one who abide as Inner Controller of all beings or facing you in the ways of Kṣattriyas, I shall pay off the debt of my manes.”

devanagari: एवं रूक्षैस्तुदन्वाक्यैः कृष्णं तोत्रैरिव द्विपम् । गदयाताडयन्मूर्ध्नि सिंहवद्व्यनदच्च सः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ rūkṣaistudanvākyaiḥ kṛṣṇaṃ totrairiva dvipam gadayātāḍayanmūrdhni siṃhavadvyanadacca saḥ

translation: Cutting to the quick Kṛṣṇa, with such harsh words like piercing an elephant with a goad, he hit Kṛṣṇa on the head with his mace and roared like a lion.

devanagari: गदयाभिहतोऽप्याजौ न चचाल यदूद्वहः । कृष्णोऽपि तमहन्गुर्व्या कौमोदक्या स्तनान्तरे ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: gadayābhihato kṛṣṇo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Though struck down with a mace in that battle, the descendant of Yadu (Kṛṣṇa) remained unshaken. Kṛṣṇa on his part hit Dantavaktra on the chest with his mace Kaumodakī.

devanagari: गदानिर्भिन्नहृदय उद्वमन्रुधिरं मुखात् । प्रसार्य केशबाह्वङ्घ्रीन्धरण्यां न्यपतद्व्यसुः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: gadānirbhinnahṛdaya udvamanrudhiraṃ mukhāt prasārya keśabāhvaṅghrīndharaṇyāṃ nyapatadvyasuḥ
translation: With his heart shattered with the stroke of the mace, Dantavaktra vomitted blood through his mouth and he fell dead on the ground with his hair dishevelled and arms and legs outstretched.

devanagari: ततः सूक्ष्मतरं ज्योतिः कृष्णमाविशदद्भुतम् । पश्यतां सर्वभूतानां यथा चैद्यवधे नृप ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ sūkṣmataraṃ jyotiḥ kṛṣṇamāviśadadbhutam paśyatāṃ sarvabhūtānāṃ yathā caidyavadhe nṛpa
translation: Determined to strike down Kṛṣṇa in the battle the foolish fellow darted towards him like an arrow discharged from a bow.

devanagari: विदूरथस्तु तद्भ्राता भ्रातृशोकपरिप्लुतः । आगच्छदसिचर्माभ्यामुच्छ्वसंस्तज्जिघांसया ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: vidūrathastu tadbhrātā bhrātṛśokapariplutaḥ āgacchadasicarmābhyāmucchvasaṃstajjighāṃsayā
translation: Overwhelmed with grief for his brother, Vidūratha, Dantavaktra’s brother, armed with a sword and a shield, came there having heavy sighs, but desired to kill Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: तस्य चापततः कृष्णश्चक्रेण क्षुरनेमिना । शिरो जहार राजेन्द्र सकिरीटं सकुण्डलम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya cāpatataḥ kṛṣṇaścakreṇa kṣuraneminā śiro jahāra rājendra sakirīṭaṃ sakuṇḍalam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṣur class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ kṣur class 6 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: As he was advancing, Kṛṣṇa with his discus of sharp edge, severed off his head adorned with a diadem and ear-rings.

devanagari: एवं सौभं च शाल्वं च दन्तवक्रं सहानुजम् । हत्वा दुर्विषहानन्यैरीडितः सुरमानवैः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ saubhaṃ ca śālvaṃ ca dantavakraṃ sahānujam hatvā durviṣahānanyairīḍitaḥ suramānavaiḥ
translation: Kṛṣṇa again mounted his divine chariot Puṣparatha which was tastefully decorated. He was accompanied by the Yādava clansmen and was praised by gods and men (in songs).

devanagari: मुनिभिः सिद्धगन्धर्वैर्विद्याधरमहोरगैः । अप्सरोभिः पितृगणैर्यक्षैः किन्नरचारणैः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: munibhiḥ siddhagandharvairvidyādharamahoragaiḥ apsarobhiḥ pitṛgaṇairyakṣaiḥ kinnaracāraṇaiḥ
translation: Having thus killed both Dantavaktra and Vidūratha in the battle, the Lord put to flight their army and captured their elephants (horses, etc.)

devanagari: उपगीयमानविजयः कुसुमैरभिवर्षितः । वृतश्च वृष्णिप्रवरैर्विवेशालङ्कृतां पुरीम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: upagīyamānavijayaḥ kusumairabhivarṣitaḥ vṛtaśca vṛṣṇipravarairviveśālaṅkṛtāṃ purīm

translation: Kṛṣṇa again mounted his divine chariot Puṣparatha which was tastefully decorated. He was accompanied by the Yādava clansmen and was praised by gods and men (in songs).

devanagari: एवं योगेश्वरः कृष्णो भगवान्जगदीश्वरः । ईयते पशुदृष्टीनां निर्जितो जयतीति सः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ yogeśvaraḥ kṛṣṇo bhagavānjagadīśvaraḥ īyate paśudṛṣṭīnāṃ nirjito jayatīti saḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ ji class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ ji class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ ji class 1 verb], [locative single from √ ji class 1 verb]

translation:
In this way, the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa, the master of Yoga and the Almighty controller of the Universe is ever victorious, though to the ignorant (lit. persons with the vision of brutes), he appears at times vanquished.
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text adds after verse 16:]
16(A). O descendant of Bharata! The noble deeds of the Lord of hallowing renown are such as cannot be recounted in tens of thousands of years (even god Brahmā and others cannot do this, what of me?).
17(B). O chastiser of enemies! In this way the slayer of the demon Madhu (Śrī Kṛṣṇa) began (the destruction of Rākṣasas) with the demoness Pūtanā and killed all the Asuras who were a burden to the earth.
[Here ends Chapter No. 101 in Padaratnāvalī’s Text)]
[Padaratnāvalī’s introduction:]
Though the life of Balarāma is interwoven with that of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, two more chapters are added specifically to the life and deeds of Balarāma.

devanagari: श्रुत्वा युद्धोद्यमं रामः कुरूणां सह पाण्डवैः । तीर्थाभिषेकव्याजेन मध्यस्थः प्रययौ किल ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutvā yuddhodyamaṃ rāmaḥ kurūṇāṃ saha pāṇḍavaiḥ tīrthābhiṣekavyājena madhyasthaḥ prayayau kila
translation: It is reported that when Balarāma heard of the war-preparations of the Kauravas against the Pāṇḍavas, he, being neutral[4] (with reference to the belligerent parties) went away on the pretext of a pilgrimage for performing ablutions in sacred voters.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
KD. states that Balarāma, out of sympathy, wanted to intervene between the warring parties incidently, when he would reach Kurukṣetra in the course of his pilgrimage—78.146.

devanagari: स्नात्वा प्रभासे सन्तर्प्य देवर्षिपितृमानवान् । सरस्वतीं प्रतिस्रोतं ययौ ब्राह्मणसंवृतः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: snātvā prabhāse santarpya devarṣipitṛmānavān sarasvatīṃ pratisrotaṃ yayau brāhmaṇasaṃvṛtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: After taking bath at Prabhāsa[5] and propitiating gods, sages and manes (pitṛs) by offering oblations of water and satisfying men (by serving them food), Balarāma, surrounded by Brāhmaṇas, went from the mouth of the Sarasvatī[6] to its source.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Somanath or Verawal in Saurashtra. It is also called Devapattana. Somanath is one of the famous twelve jyotir-liṅgas of god Mahādeva. In its neighbourhood are the spots where Yādavas’ internecine battle and death of Kṛṣṇa took place.—GDAMI. p. 157.

Footnote [6]:
It appears that at that time, the Sarasvatī was still flowing into the Gulf of Cutch i.e. The Arabian sea. There is a small river Rauṇākṣī, also known as the Sarasvatī in Vāmana P. (ch. 84) which falls in the sea near Prabhāsa. But the later description shows that Balarāma followed the course of the Vedic Sarasvatī to its source.

devanagari: पृथूदकं बिन्दुसरस्त्रितकूपं सुदर्शनम् । विशालं ब्रह्मतीर्थं च चक्रं प्राचीं सरस्वतीम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: pṛthūdakaṃ bindusarastritakūpaṃ sudarśanam viśālaṃ brahmatīrthaṃ ca cakraṃ prācīṃ sarasvatīm
translation: [7] (On the way) he visited various holy places like Pṛthūdaka[8], Bindusaras[9], Tritakūpa[10], Sudarśana[11], Viśāla, Brahmatīrtha,[12] Cakratīrtha and the spot where the Sarasvatī flows to the east[13].
purport:
Footnote [7]:
It appears that no geographical sequence is followed in the description of this pilgrimage.

Footnote [8]:
Pehoa in the Karnal district, Panjab. It is 14 miles to the west of Thanesvar. This Tīrtha is on the Sarasvatī, near the famous (Brahmayoni Tīrtha—GDAMI, p. 16. Vainya performed nine aśvamedha sacrifices at Pṛthūdaka (VT).

Footnote [9]:
VT. states this to be the hermitage of the sage Kardama near Siddhapura in Gujarat. N.L. De identifies it with the same: Sitpur to the northwest of Ahmedabad—GDAMI, p. 38.

Footnote [10]:
Tritakūpa—A place near the Sarasvatī. In that well Trita fell and was taken out of it by god Soma (VT). Probably this refers to the incident supposed to be the basis of Ṛg Veda Saṃhitā. 1.105.

Footnote [11]:
In Kurukṣetra, the same as Rāmahrada—a sacred tank to the north of Thanesvar. Bhāvāratha Dīpikā identifies Sudarśana and Cakra Tirtha.—GDAMI, p. 43, 166.

Footnote [12]:
A certain Tirtha on the Sarasvatī.

Footnote [13]:
The Kurukṣetra Sarasvatī is called Prācī or Eastern Sarasvatī (Padma-purāṇa uttara khaṇḍa (ch. 67). N.L. De states that this name is specially- applied to the Sarasvatī that issues out of the Puṣkara Lake—GDAMI, p. 187.

devanagari: यमुनामनु यान्येव गङ्गामनु च भारत । जगाम नैमिषं यत्र ऋषयः सत्रमासते ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: yamunāmanu yānyeva gaṅgāmanu ca bhārata jagāma naimiṣaṃ yatra ṛṣayaḥ satramāsate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: And passing through the various sacred places which were on the Yamunā and the Gaṅgā, he went to the Naimiṣa forest[14] where sages were holding a prolonged sacrificial session.
purport:
Footnote [14]:
Modern Nimsar on the left bank of the Gomatī in the Sitapur district of U.P. It is 20 miles to the North-West of Lucknow.—GDAMR p. 135.

devanagari: तमागतमभिप्रेत्य मुनयो दीर्घसत्रिणः । अभिनन्द्य यथान्यायं प्रणम्योत्थाय चार्चयन् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tamāgatamabhipretya munayo dīrghasatriṇaḥ abhinandya yathānyāyaṃ praṇamyotthāya cārcayan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ṛc ], [vocative single from √ ṛc ]
[nominative single from √ ṛc ], [vocative single from √ ṛc ]

translation: Having come to know that the distinguished guest was Balarāma, the sages who were engaged in the prolonged sacrifice, received him with due formalities, rose from their seats, bowed down to him and worshipped him.

devanagari: सोऽर्चितः सपरीवारः कृतासनपरिग्रहः । रोमहर्षणमासीनं महर्षेः शिष्यमैक्षत ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: so romaharṣaṇamāsīnaṃ maharṣeḥ śiṣyamaikṣata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śās class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ śās class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ śiṣ class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ śiṣ class 10 verb]
[imperfect middle third single], [aorist middle third plural]

translation: Balarāma who was thus worshipped, took his seat along with his followers. He noticed that Romaharṣaṇa, the disciple of the great sage Vyāsa, remained seated there, occupying a higher seat in front of the Brāhmaṇas.

devanagari: अप्रत्युत्थायिनं सूतमकृतप्रह्वणाञ्जलिम् । अध्यासीनं च तान्विप्रांश्चुकोपोद्वीक्ष्य माधवः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: apratyutthāyinaṃ sūtamakṛtaprahvaṇāñjalim adhyāsīnaṃ ca tānviprāṃścukopodvīkṣya mādhavaḥ

[aorist active second plural], [aorist middle third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Balarāma, the descendant of Madhu, got enraged when he found that Romahaṛṣaṇa, a sūta, did not rise from his seat to show reverence to him after his arrival, nor did he fold his palms to bow him and continued to occupy a higher seat in the presence of the Brāhmaṇas.

devanagari: यस्मादसाविमान्विप्रानध्यास्ते प्रतिलोमजः । धर्मपालांस्तथैवास्मान्वधमर्हति दुर्मतिः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: yasmādasāvimānviprānadhyāste pratilomajaḥ dharmapālāṃstathaivāsmānvadhamarhati durmatiḥ
translation: (He said to the Brāhmaṇas) “Why does this Sūta, a child of irregular marriage (between a Kṣattriya father and a Brāhmaṇa (mother) occupied a seat higher than Brāhmaṇas and us who are the protectors of religion and social code of conduct. This evil-minded fellow deserves to be killed.

devanagari: ऋषेर्भगवतो भूत्वा शिष्योऽधीत्य बहूनि च । सेतिहासपुराणानि धर्मशास्त्राणि सर्वशः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣerbhagavato bhūtvā śiṣyo setihāsapurāṇāni dharmaśāstrāṇi sarvaśaḥ
translation: In spite of being a disciple of that worshipful sage Vyāsa and having studied many smṛtis (codes of righteous and religious conduct—social and personal) along with all the works on Itihāsa and purāṇaṣ, he lacks self control, has no sense of discipline or modesty; he vainly pretends to be a learned man. His scholarship and learning do not contribute to his real excellence as in the case of an actor who lacks control over his mind and senses.

devanagari: अदान्तस्याविनीतस्य वृथा पण्डितमानिनः । न गुणाय भवन्ति स्म नटस्येवाजितात्मनः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: adāntasyāvinītasya vṛthā paṇḍitamāninaḥ na guṇāya bhavanti sma naṭasyevājitātmanaḥ
translation: In spite of being a disciple of that worshipful sage Vyāsa and having studied many smṛtis (codes of righteous and religious conduct—social and personal) along with all the works on Itihāsa and purāṇaṣ, he lacks self control, has no sense of discipline or modesty; he vainly pretends to be a learned man. His scholarship and learning do not contribute to his real excellence as in the case of an actor who lacks control over his mind and senses.

devanagari: एतदर्थो हि लोकेऽस्मिन्नवतारो मया कृतः । वध्या मे धर्मध्वजिनस्ते हि पातकिनोऽधिकाः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: etadartho hi loke vadhyā me dharmadhvajinaste hi pātakino

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: It is with this specific purpose that I have taken incarnation in this world, viz. those imposters and hypocrites who merely pretend to be religious should be done away with by me; for such people are more sinful than the unrighteous.”

devanagari: एतावदुक्त्वा भगवान्निवृत्तोऽसद्वधादपि । भावित्वात्तं कुशाग्रेण करस्थेनाहनत्प्रभुः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: etāvaduktvā bhagavānnivṛtto bhāvitvāttaṃ kuśāgreṇa karasthenāhanatprabhuḥ
translation: Balarāma had desisted from slaying even the wicked ones (as he was a pilgrim then). But as fate would have it, the mighty Lord spoke these words and pierced the Sūta with the end of a Kuśa grass.

devanagari: हाहेतिवादिनः सर्वे मुनयः खिन्नमानसाः । ऊचुः सङ्कर्षणं देवमधर्मस्ते कृतः प्रभो ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: hāhetivādinaḥ sarve munayaḥ khinnamānasāḥ ūcuḥ saṅkarṣaṇaṃ devamadharmaste kṛtaḥ prabho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Terrified and dismayed in their minds, all the sages screamed out exclaiming, “Alas! Alas”. They said to Balarāma, “O Lord! You have perpetrated an irreligious and unrighteous deed.

devanagari: अस्य ब्रह्मासनं दत्तमस्माभिर्यदुनन्दन । आयुश्चात्माक्लमं तावद्यावत्सत्रं समाप्यते ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: asya brahmāsanaṃ dattamasmābhiryadunandana āyuścātmāklamaṃ tāvadyāvatsatraṃ samāpyate
translation: O descendant of Yadu! This elevated seat of a Brāhmaṇa has been offered by us to him as also, a long period of life and indefatiguate body till the sacrificial session lasts.

devanagari: अजानतैवाचरितस्त्वया ब्रह्मवधो यथा । योगेश्वरस्य भवतो नाम्नायोऽपि नियामकः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: ajānataivācaritastvayā brahmavadho yathā yogeśvarasya bhavato nāmnāyo
translation: May be unknowingly but you have committed what amounts to be the sin of killing a Brāhmaṇa. (It is true that) being a master of Yoga, even the Vedic injunction against killing a Brāhmaṇa is not binding on you.

devanagari: यद्येतद्ब्रह्महत्यायाः पावनं लोकपावन । चरिष्यति भवांल्लोक सङ्ग्रहोऽनन्यचोदितः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: yadyetadbrahmahatyāyāḥ pāvanaṃ lokapāvana cariṣyati bhavāṃlloka saṅgraho
translation: O purifier of the world! If your worship, however, performs, of your own accord, the expiation for the sin of Brahmahatyā without being prompted to do so by others, you will be setting a noble example to others.[15]
purport:
Footnote [15]:
yad yad ācarati śreṣṭhas tat tad evetaro janaḥ /
sa yat pramāṇaṃ kurute lokas tad anuvartate //
  —Bhagavad Gītā3.2

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । चरिष्ये वधनिर्वेशं लोकानुग्रहकाम्यया । नियमः प्रथमे कल्पे यावान्स तु विधीयताम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca cariṣye vadhanirveśaṃ lokānugrahakāmyayā niyamaḥ prathame kalpe yāvānsa tu vidhīyatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The glorious Lord Balarāma said:
I shall expiate for this act of Brāhmaṇa-cide in order tc set an example to the world. Be pleased to prescribe to me the best course of atonement for such an act.

devanagari: दीर्घमायुर्बतैतस्य सत्त्वमिन्द्रि यमेव च । आशासितं यत्तद्ब्रूते साधये योगमायया ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: dīrghamāyurbataitasya sattvamindri yameva ca āśāsitaṃ yattadbrūte sādhaye yogamāyayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Be pleased to tell me whatever is expected by you for him—such as a long span of life, strength, sound-ness of sense-organs—and I shall accomplish it for him by my Yogic potency.

devanagari: ऋषय ऊचुः । अस्त्रस्य तव वीर्यस्य मृत्योरस्माकमेव च । यथा भवेद्वचः सत्यं तथा राम विधीयताम् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣaya ūcuḥ astrasya tava vīryasya mṛtyorasmākameva ca yathā bhavedvacaḥ satyaṃ tathā rāma vidhīyatām
translation:
The sages replied:
O Balarāma! May you be pleased to act in such a way as will preserve the infallibility of your missile (charged in the Kuśa grass), the efficacy of your prowess, the inevitability of death and that our boon (that the sūta should narrate the purāṇas to the end of our sacrificial session) also may not prove untrue.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । आत्मा वै पुत्र उत्पन्न इति वेदानुशासनम् । तस्मादस्य भवेद्वक्ता आयुरिन्द्रि यसत्त्ववान् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca ātmā vai putra utpanna iti vedānuśāsanam tasmādasya bhavedvaktā āyurindri yasattvavān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative plural from √ vaj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vaj class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vaj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vaj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vaj class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vak class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vak class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vak class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vak class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vak class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ vā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ vā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vā class 1 verb]

translation:
Lord Balarāma said:
It is declared by the Vedas that it is one’s own self that is born as a son.[16] His son Ugraśravas will now be the expounder of the Purāṇas and he will be blessed with a long life, bodily strength and keenness of senses (and intelligence).
purport:
Footnote [16]:
aṅgād aṅgāt sambhavasi hṛdayād abhijāyase /
ātmā vai putra-nāmāsi sa jīva śaradaḥ śatam //
  —Kauṣitaki Up. 2.7.
Balarāma exhorts the sages to install Ugraśravas in the place of Romaharṣaṇa and all the boons promised to him by the sages will be now effective in the case of the son of Romaharṣaṇa.

devanagari: किं वः कामो मुनिश्रेष्ठा ब्रूताहं करवाण्यथ । अजानतस्त्वपइतिं यथा मे चिन्त्यतां बुधाः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ vaḥ kāmo muniśreṣṭhā brūtāhaṃ karavāṇyatha ajānatastvapaitiṃ yathā me cintyatāṃ budhāḥ
translation: O great sages! Be pleased to tell me what else you wish to have and I shall accomplish it for you. O learned sages! Please consider and advise me the course of expiation for the sin committed by me unknowingly.

devanagari: ऋषय ऊचुः । इल्वलस्य सुतो घोरो बल्वलो नाम दानवः । स दूषयति नः सत्रमेत्य पर्वणि पर्वणि ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣaya ūcuḥ ilvalasya suto ghoro balvalo nāma dānavaḥ sa dūṣayati naḥ satrametya parvaṇi parvaṇi
translation:
The sages said:
There is a terrible Dānava, named Balvala, the son of Ilvala. On every parva (full-moon and new-moon) days, he comes here and pollutes the sanctity of our sacrifice.

devanagari: तं पापं जहि दाशार्ह तन्नः शुश्रूषणं परम् । पूयशोणितविन्मूत्र सुरामांसाभिवर्षिणम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ pāpaṃ jahi dāśārha tannaḥ śuśrūṣaṇaṃ param pūyaśoṇitavinmūtra surāmāṃsābhivarṣiṇam
translation: He showers (on the sacred sacrifice) puss, blood, faeces, urine, wine and flesh. Please kill that wicked demon and that will be the most valuable service rendered to us by you, O descendant of Daśārha.

devanagari: ततश्च भारतं वर्षं परीत्य सुसमाहितः । चरित्वा द्वादशमासांस्तीर्थस्नायी विशुध्यसि ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: tataśca bhārataṃ varṣaṃ parītya susamāhitaḥ caritvā dvādaśamāsāṃstīrthasnāyī viśudhyasi
translation:
Thereafter, for twelve months you go round the Bhāratavarṣa and with a serene mind take bath in the sacred waters. Thereby being absolved of the sin, you will be purified.
[back to top]
